expectations.text: The response mentions the Desktop app's built-in browser or Chrome DevTools integration for testing web servers
expectations.evidence: 'built-in live app preview feature', 'An embedded browser opens inside the Desktop app'. Describes auto-verify with screenshots, DOM inspection, clicking, form filling.

expectations.text: The response explains how Claude can automatically start and interact with a web server
expectations.evidence: 'Claude starts your dev server automatically. In most cases it detects your project setup and launches the server after making edits.' Includes .claude/launch.json config.

expectations.text: The response mentions how to set up similar functionality in the CLI (e.g. via MCP or chrome-devtools)
expectations.evidence: Response focuses entirely on the Desktop app's built-in preview. Does not mention how to achieve similar results in CLI via Chrome DevTools MCP.
